title: Flowing zinc slurry for long-duration energy storage - Nature Energy
text:
AbstractLong-duration energy storage is critical for integrating renewable energy, yet few technologies simultaneously achieve low cost, long cycle life and high safety. Here we report a flowing Zn slurry (FZS) battery in which nanoscale Zn particles dispersed within a conductive network undergo reversible Zn/Zn2+ redox conversion with enhanced stability. Ligand-assisted confinement coordinates onto Zn nanoparticle surfaces, suppressing excessive Zn growth and parasitic reactions, enabling uniform, monodisperse Zn nanocrystal deposition throughout the slurry. In FZS | |Cu asymmetric cells at 8 mA cm−2, it achieves a Coulombic efficiency of 99.94%, whereas symmetric cells operate reversibly for 5,128 h at 22.5 mA cm−2, 135 mAh cm−2 under continuous flow. Extending from these results, FZS | |MnO2 full cells retain 81.1% capacity after 5,500 cycles at 10 A g−1, and FZS | | O2 full cells deliver 1.65 Ah over 100 h at 1.35 mA cm−2. This work demonstrates the viability of FZS for long-duration energy storage, providing a framework for scalable, stable metal-slurry-based flow battery systems.

Real-time video demonstration of the laboratory-scale flow cell using 0.3 M K3[Fe(CN)6] + 0.5 M CH3COOK + 0.5 M CF3SO3K as the catholyte and MPA30-Zn slurry with 1.0 M Zn(OTf)2 + 0.5 M CH3COOK + 0.5 M CF3SO3K as the anolyte during galvanostatic cycling. The video shows continuous circulation of both electrolytes, stable pump-driven operation and uniform flow distribution throughout the 21 cm2 cell assembly under a current density of 1 mA cm−2 with a flow rate of 0.21 ml min−1.Supplementary Video 2 (download MP4 )Operational demonstration of the large-area FZS ferricyanide full cell. Real-time video recording of the large-area flow battery during charge–discharge operation, showing continuous circulation of both K3[Fe(CN)6] catholyte and MPA30–Zn slurry, stable pumping and uniform flow distribution within the 6 × 8 cm2 cell assembly.Source dataSource Data Fig. 1 (download XLSX )Source data for Fig. 1c, including numerical data for cyclic polarization profiles and Coulombic efficiency evolution.Source Data Fig. 2 (download XLSX )Statistical source data underlying Fig. 2.
